bullets.text: Q1 2014 combined ratio was 93.4 despite wild winter weather; monthly variation was significant, including a February result briefly above 96, followed by a sub-92 March, both within seasonally adjusted predictive ranges.
Written premium grew just over 5% for the quarter; winter weather contributed to higher frequency but results were manageable within pricing across Personal Lines and Commercial Lines.
Direct new applications grew 13% and Agency grew 2% for the quarter; retention metrics returned slightly above prior-year in Direct and slightly below in Agency, with policy life expectancy building.
Progressive Home Advantage property written premium grew 44% year-over-year; Snapshot premium volume was up 31% with 110 terabytes of vehicle-provided data now available for analysis.

Mobile quoting and servicing volumes showed strong growth; a unified quoting experience platform launch was a notable Q1 technology delivery enabling consistent consumer experience across all devices.
Agency channel new applications showed high volatility, with strong late-February growth moderating after tax refund timing shifts and possible agency closures during extreme cold weather periods.

Investment total return was 1.4% for the quarter (~$150 million comprehensive income); portfolio duration was a short 1.7 years as management continued resisting yield-reaching at uncomfortable risk levels.
Commercial Lines finished at a sub-91 combined ratio despite a 17-point monthly swing; management highlighted continued refinement of Service Centers co-located with claim offices.
